original_image_tags: Scan of the reverse side of a document page. The paper is aged, beige, with two punched holes at the top and a piece of yellowed tape at the upper right edge. The page is largely blank but contains faint, reversed text bleeding through from the front side, mostly illegible. At the bottom, several blue and black stamped notations are visible, oriented upside down relative to the page's orientation. The stamps indicate receipt and processing by various FBI divisions.

elements.description: Multiple overlapping blue ink stamps on an aged, off-white page with punch holes and adhesive tape. The stamps contain reversed (mirror-image) text. The most legible lines at the bottom read 'FBI LABORATORY DIVISION', 'RECEIVER', and several dates and times such as 'Sep 5 2 44 PM '47' and 'SEP 5 2 27 PM '47'. Other stamped text includes 'FBI', 'TECHNICAL LABORATOR', 'RECEIVED-HARBO', 'RECEIVED-LADD', and 'U.S. DEPT OF JUSTICE'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content of an aged page with punched holes, tape, and multiple blue stamps, but Description B adds specific quoted stamp text that Description A omits, without contradiction.
